mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, reducing present values and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, it signals severe labor market deterioration and weak consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or t-test p-value exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicates strengthening residential demand and construction activity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through correlated sector earnings.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: A rising index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which supports corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during rising industrial production periods show no statistically significant positive drift compared to baseline, or Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP demonstrates broad-based economic expansion, which increases aggregate corporate profits and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth periods yield negative or flat 60-day forward returns in over half of observed quarters, failing to reject the null hypothesis of zero alpha.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.5 percent signals accelerating inflation that prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following high inflation prints are not significantly negative, or the signal loses predictive power with a Rank IC below 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint pattern of rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment indicates a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between payrolls and unemployment fails to produce a positive Rank IC above 0.04, or the joint signal predicts negative returns in most instances.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts increase by more than 5 percent month-over-month, it signals a robust housing cycle that amplifies demand for related goods and services, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts above the 5 percent threshold do not precede positive 20-day returns in at least 60 percent of cases, or the signal predictive accuracy degrades below random chance.
